specification.topic-statement: The OpenAI API routing system selects between the Chat Completions API and the Responses API based on the model identifier, and maps thinking levels to provider-specific reasoning effort strings for each model.

scope.in-scope: The hardcoded model classification tables that determine which API endpoint is used, the reasoning model designation, and the per-model thinking-level-to-effort-string mappings.
scope.boundary: The structural differences between Chat Completions and Responses API request bodies are covered by the provider serialization specification. This specification covers only which API path is chosen and what effort string is produced for a given model and thinking level.

behavior.api-selection:
Every request to the OpenAI provider is routed to one of two API endpoints. The selection is determined by looking up the model identifier in a hardcoded list of Responses API models:
The following model identifiers are routed to the Responses API (`/v1/responses`):
- `o1`, `o1-mini`, `o1-preview` - `o3`, `o3-mini`, `o3-pro` - `o4-mini` - `gpt-5`, `gpt-5-mini`, `gpt-5-nano`, `gpt-5-pro` - `gpt-5.1`, `gpt-5.1-chat-latest`, `gpt-5.1-codex` - `gpt-5.2`, `gpt-5.2-chat-latest`, `gpt-5.2-codex`
All other model identifiers — including any not present in the table — are routed to the Chat Completions API (`/v1/chat/completions`).
Lookup is by exact string comparison. A model such as `gpt-5.0` or `o3-medium` does not match any entry and silently routes to Chat Completions, even if it is semantically a reasoning model.
The provider instance records a `use_responses_api` flag at creation time. When that flag is set, every request through that instance is forced to the Responses API regardless of the model identifier. When the flag is not set, the model-identifier lookup governs the choice per request.
behavior.reasoning-model-designation:
A model is a reasoning model if and only if its identifier appears in a hardcoded list:
- `o1`, `o1-mini`, `o1-preview` - `o3`, `o3-mini`, `o3-pro` - `o4-mini` - `gpt-5`, `gpt-5-mini`, `gpt-5-nano`, `gpt-5-pro` - `gpt-5.1`, `gpt-5.1-chat-latest`, `gpt-5.1-codex` - `gpt-5.2`, `gpt-5.2-chat-latest`, `gpt-5.2-codex`
The reasoning model list and the Responses API model list contain the same entries. A model is a reasoning model if and only if it uses the Responses API.
Reasoning model designation affects whether a reasoning effort parameter is included in the request. For non-reasoning models no effort parameter is ever added.
behavior.reasoning-effort-mapping:
For reasoning models, a thinking level is translated to a per-model effort string that is passed in the request. Each model has its own mapping:
Model-specific effort strings by thinking level (MIN / LOW / MED / HIGH):
- `o1`: `"low"` / `"low"` / `"medium"` / `"high"` (MIN and LOW are identical; no lower effort available) - `o1-mini`: `"low"` / `"low"` / `"medium"` / `"high"` - `o1-preview`: `"low"` / `"low"` / `"medium"` / `"high"` - `o3`: `"none"` / `"low"` / `"medium"` / `"high"` (thinking can be disabled with `"none"` at MIN) - `o3-mini`: `"none"` / `"low"` / `"medium"` / `"high"` - `o3-pro`: `"low"` / `"low"` / `"medium"` / `"high"` - `o4-mini`: `"none"` / `"low"` / `"medium"` / `"high"` - `gpt-5`: `"minimal"` / `"low"` / `"medium"` / `"high"` (MIN uses `"minimal"`, distinct from `"none"`) - `gpt-5-mini`: `"minimal"` / `"low"` / `"medium"` / `"high"` - `gpt-5-nano`: `"none"` / `"low"` / `"medium"` / `"high"` - `gpt-5-pro`: `"none"` / `"low"` / `"medium"` / `"high"` - `gpt-5.1`: `"minimal"` / `"low"` / `"medium"` / `"high"` - `gpt-5.1-chat-latest`: `"medium"` / `"medium"` / `"medium"` / `"medium"` (all four levels produce the same effort; this model is always medium) - `gpt-5.1-codex`: `"none"` / `"low"` / `"medium"` / `"high"` - `gpt-5.2`: `"none"` / `"low"` / `"medium"` / `"xhigh"` (HIGH uses `"xhigh"`, a super-high effort level) - `gpt-5.2-chat-latest`: `"medium"` / `"medium"` / `"high"` / `"xhigh"` - `gpt-5.2-codex`: `"none"` / `"low"` / `"medium"` / `"xhigh"`
Regardless of the configured thinking level, this model always receives `"medium"` effort. The level selection has no effect.
The `"xhigh"` string is sent verbatim to the API for certain gpt-5.2 models at HIGH thinking level. Whether the API accepts this value is determined by the API, not by this implementation.
If a model is designated as a reasoning model but its identifier does not appear in the effort mapping table, no effort parameter is included in the request. The model receives a Responses API request with no reasoning configuration.
